title: Raw resting-state EEG dataset with alternating eyes-open and eyes-closed recordings in healthy adults
description: This dataset comprises raw resting-state EEG recordings from healthy adults acquired using a 128-channel Biosemi ActiveTwo system at Universidad Autónoma de Madrid. Participants completed four consecutive 5-minute runs alternating between eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions within a single ~20-minute session. The dataset is organized by condition (eyes-open and eyes-closed) rather than by visit, with no preprocessing applied prior to sharing.
methods_description: EEG recordings were acquired using a Biosemi ActiveTwo 128-channel system at 1024 Hz sampling rate (2048 Hz for some subjects) in a Faraday cage under dim lighting (120-150 lux). Participants completed four 5-minute runs in alternating eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions, with randomized acquisition sequence. Participants were instructed to sit still, avoid muscle tension, and fixate on a black point for eyes-open recordings.
